tool.name: KneadData
tool.description: Quality control and contaminant removal for metagenomic data
tool.command:
mkdir -p results/ ;
kneaddata #if $read_type.select_read_type == "single" --unpaired "$read_type.single_read" #else -i1 "$read_type.forward_read" -i2 "$read_type.backward_read" #end if -o results/ --log-level "$log_level" #if $alignment.alignment_tool.tool_choice == "bmtagger" --run-bmtagger #if $alignment.alignment_tool.reference_genome.source == "indexed" --reference-db "$alignment.alignment_tool.reference_genome.bmtagger_db.fields.path" #else --reference-db "$alignment.alignment_tool.reference_genome.own_file" #end if #else #if $alignment.alignment_tool.bowtie2_options.select_bowtie2_option == "custom" --bowtie2-options "$alignment.alignment_tool.bowtie2_options.custom_bowtie2_settings" #end if #if $alignment.alignment_tool.reference_genome.source == "indexed" --reference-db "$alignment.alignment_tool.reference_genome.bowtie2_db.fields.path" #else --reference-db "$alignment.alignment_tool.reference_genome.own_file" #end if #end if --quality-scores "$quality_scores" #if $trimmomatic.trimmomatic_step.decide_trimmomatic == "run_trimmomatic" #if $trimmomatic.trimmomatic_step.trimmomatic_options.select_option == "custom" --trimmomatic-options "$trimmomatic.trimmomatic_step.trimmomatic_options.custom_settings" #end if #if $trimmomatic.trimmomatic_step.sequencer --sequencer-source "$trimmomatic.trimmomatic_step.sequencer" #end if #else --bypass-trim #end if #if $tandem.trf_step.trf_bool == "include" --mismatch "$tandem.trf_step.trf.mismatch" --delta "$tandem.trf_step.trf.indel" --minscore "$tandem.trf_step.trf.minimum_score" --maxperiod "$tandem.trf_step.trf.maximum_period" #else --bypass-trf #end if #if $fastqc.fastqc_report.select_fastqc == "include_fastqc" $fastqc.fastqc_report.fastqc_start $fastqc.fastqc_report.fastqc_end #end if $fastqc.trim_repetitive $store_temp $cat_final_output

param.name: select_read_type
param.label: Read type
param.help: Specify whether your sequencing data is single-end (one file) or paired-end (two files).

param.name: quality_scores
param.label: Select quality score format
param.help: Select the quality score encoding format used in your FASTQ files. Phred33 is the default and correct for most modern sequencing data.

param.name: tool_choice
param.label: Select alignment tool for host removal
param.help: Bowtie2 is the default. As an alternative BMTagger can be chosen as the alignment tool.

param.name: select_bowtie2_option
param.label: Bowtie2 alignment configuration
param.help: Default settings use Bowtie2's '--very-sensitive' mode for host read removal. Custom settings allow you to override alignment parameters like sensitivity, mismatches, or alignment mode.

param.name: custom_bowtie2_settings
param.label: Custom Bowtie2 command-line options
param.help: Specify additional Bowtie2 parameters. Example: '--very-fast' , '-p 2'

param.name: source
param.label: Select reference database source
param.help: Select a built-in genome index or upload your own FASTA file

param.name: bowtie2_db
param.label: Select reference genome database
param.help: Select the organism genome to filter out. This genome's reads will be removed as host contamination.

param.name: source
param.label: Select reference database source
param.help: Select a built-in genome index or upload your own FASTA file

param.name: bmtagger_db
param.label: Select reference genome database
param.help: Select the organism genome to filter out. This genome's reads will be removed as host contamination.

param.name: select_fastqc
param.label: FastQC quality reports
param.help: FastQC generates HTML reports with quality metrics for your sequencing data.

param.name: fastqc_start
param.label: Run FastQC on input reads
param.help: Generate FastQC report on input reads before processing

param.name: fastqc_end
param.label: Run FastQC on cleaned reads
param.help: Generate FastQC report on cleaned reads after processing

param.name: trim_repetitive
param.label: Trim repetitive/overrepresented sequences
param.help: Automatically trim sequences that FastQC flags as overrepresented. This doesn't generate HTML reports. Can be used with or without FastQC HTML reports.

param.name: decide_trimmomatic
param.label: Run Trimmomatic quality/adapter trimming
param.help: Trimmomatic performs quality-based trimming and adapter removal. Recommended for most datasets to remove low-quality bases and sequencing adapters.

param.name: select_option
param.label: Trimmomatic parameters
param.help: Default: SLIDINGWINDOW:4:20 and MINLEN:50. Selecting custom lets you override these parameters.

param.name: custom_settings
param.label: Custom Trimmomatic settings
param.help: Manually specifying additional arguments will completely override the defaults.

param.name: sequencer
param.label: Available sequencers
param.help: Choose the adapter set that matches your sequencing library preparation method.

param.name: trf_bool
param.label: Specify if you want to include the TRF step in the workflow.
param.help: TRF detects and masks tandem repeats to prevent false positive alignments in repetitive genomic regions.

param.name: mismatch
param.label: Mismatch penalty
param.help: Penalty for base mismatches in repeat detection. Higher values make detection less permissive (fewer repeats reported). Lower values are more permissive.

param.name: indel
param.label: Indel penalty (delta)
param.help: Penalty for insertions/deletions in repeat detection. Higher values are less permissive, requiring perfect repeats. Lower values allow gaps in repeats.

param.name: minimum_score
param.label: Minimum alignment score
param.help: Minimum score required to report a tandem repeat. Repeats with scores below this value are ignored.

param.name: maximum_period
param.label: Maximum period size to report
param.help: Maximum length of repeating unit to detect (in base pairs).

param.name: log_level
param.label: Logging level
param.help: Controls how much detail is written to the log file. DEBUG: All messages. INFO: Standard messages. WARNING: Only warnings and errors. ERROR and CRITICAL: Only severe issues.

param.name: store_temp
param.label: Keep temporary files
param.help: Save temporary/intermediate files generated during processing. This includes alignment files and trimmed intermediate reads. This will increase the output file size.

param.name: cat_final_output
param.label: Merge output files
param.help: Combines all final output reads into a single file.

data.name: single_output
data.label: KneadData single end results (with TRF)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "single" and tandem["trf_step"]["trf_bool"] == "include" and not cat_final_output

data.name: single_output_trimmed
data.label: KneadData single end results (without TRF)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "single" and tandem["trf_step"]["trf_bool"] == "skip" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_forward
data.label: KneadData paired end forward reads (with TRF)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and tandem["trf_step"]["trf_bool"] == "include" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_forward_trimmed
data.label: KneadData paired end forward reads (without TRF)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and tandem["trf_step"]["trf_bool"] == "skip" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_backward
data.label: KneadData paired end reverse reads (with TRF)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and tandem["trf_step"]["trf_bool"] == "include" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_backward_trimmed
data.label: KneadData paired end reverse reads (without TRF)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and tandem["trf_step"]["trf_bool"] == "skip" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_clean_forward_bowtie
data.label: Paired forward clean reads (Bowtie2)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bowtie2" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_clean_reverse_bowtie
data.label: Paired reverse clean reads (Bowtie2)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bowtie2" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_clean_forward_bmtagger
data.label: Paired forward clean reads (BMTagger)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bmtagger" and not cat_final_output

data.name: paired_clean_reverse_bmtagger
data.label: Paired reverse clean reads (BMTagger)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bmtagger" and not cat_final_output

data.name: unmatched_clean_forward_bowtie
data.label: Unmatched forward clean reads (Bowtie2)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bowtie2" and not cat_final_output

data.name: unmatched_clean_reverse_bowtie
data.label: Unmatched reverse clean reads (Bowtie2)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bowtie2" and not cat_final_output

data.name: unmatched_clean_forward_bmtagger
data.label: Unmatched forward clean reads (BMTagger)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bmtagger" and not cat_final_output

data.name: unmatched_clean_reverse_bmtagger
data.label: Unmatched reverse clean reads (BMTagger)
data.filter: read_type["select_read_type"] == "paired" and store_temp and alignment["alignment_tool"]["tool_choice"] == "bmtagger" and not cat_final_output

tool.help:
KneadData is a tool for quality control and contaminant removal of metagenomic and metatranscriptomic sequencing data.
KneadData processes sequencing data through the following pipeline: it starts with adapter removal and quality trimming using Trimmomatic, then is followed by filtering host-derived reads through alignment to a reference genome with Bowtie2 or optionally with BMTagger. As an optional step, it can also identify and remove tandem repeats by using TRF and generate quality control reports with FastQC at the beginning and/or end of processing.
**Input Requirements**
KneadData accepts sequencing reads in standard FASTQ format. Users can provide either: - A single FASTQ file for single-end sequencing data - Two paired FASTQ files (forward and reverse reads) for paired-end data
It is also required to select a reference genome for host removal. This can be done by: - Choosing a built-in genome index from the dropdown menu - Uploading your own FASTA file
**Results and Outputs**
The output files from KneadData depend on the settings that have been chosen in the tool. Here an overview of the outputs:
Main Output Files: Will include the cleaned reads. File names contain either '_kneaddata.fastq' (if TRF was used) or '_kneaddata.trimmed.fastq' (if TRF was skipped). For single-end data, this is one file. For paired-end data, you will get separate files for forward and reverse reads. Aditionally, a log file is always included.
Contaminant Reads: These are the reads that were identified as host contamination. They are saved in separate files named with the alignment tool used: '_bowtie2_contam.fastq' for Bowtie2 or '_bmtagger_contam.fastq' for BMTagger.
Unmatched Reads: For paired-end data, when only one read of a pair passes filtering, the unmatched read is saved separately. These files are named '_kneaddata_unmatched_1.fastq' for forward reads and '_kneaddata_unmatched_2.fastq' for reverse reads.
If Concatenate Final Output Option is selected, all cleaned reads will be combined into one file '_kneaddata.fastq'. This option merges paired, unmatched, and single-end reads together.
If Store Temporary Files Option is selected, additional intermediate files from the Trimmomatic and alignment steps will be saved. These include clean intermediate files '_bowtie2_clean.fastq' and '_bowtie2_paired_clean_.fastq', which show reads after alignment.
